title: Sensor-driven control strategies for post-stroke shoulder rehabilitation exoskeletons: A systematic review
author: Karasheva, Saudanbekova, et al.

steps.title: Data extraction and classification framework
steps.instruction: A structured data extraction process was employed to analyze and compare the selected studies. Each article was reviewed and coded using a standardized framework across the following categories:-Sensor Types: Electromyography (EMG), Inertial Measurement Units (IMU), Force/Torque Sensors, Kinematic Sensors, Multimodal Sensor Fusion-Control Approaches: Force- and Admittance-Based Interaction Control, Adaptive and Assist-as-Needed (AAN) Control, Human-in-the-Loop Control, Passive Support and Gravity Compensation Control, Machine Learning-Based Predictive Control-Targeted Limb Region: Shoulder OR Full upper-limb-Actuation Method: Motor-driven, Cable-driven, Passive (e.g., spring-operated), Soft exosuits-Clinical Evaluation: Participant group (stroke patients, healthy subjects, etc.), Sample size, Outcome measures (e.g., Fugl-Meyer Assessment, ARAT, ROM, usability feedback), Study setting (laboratory, hospital, home)
